title: Go 1.26 and AWS SDK for Go v2 Client Contract · Runtime Contract: Sdk Error Interface
summary: A service copies an SDK v1 tutorial, floats module versions, shares request options incorrectly, and treats generated Go types as runtime validation. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the sdk error interface increment in pinned Go and AWS SDK v2 client contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Inspect smithy operation, API, transport, cancellation, and response errors with errors.Is and errors.As while preserving request evidence.
Apply per-operation functional options without mutating shared client configuration or leaking tenant-specific endpoints.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Sdk Error Interface · Theory Sdk Error Interface
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 9312 during a regional dependency incident. Build deterministic Go evidence to enforce pinned Go and AWS SDK v2 versions, client lifetime, runtime validation, and typed errors; record cleanup, rollback, restoration, and residual-risk ownership; then change region, range, or signed header and explain the exact failure boundary. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Sdk Error Interface contract for the supplied data.
